meaning: adj.多种子的,结籽的,破旧的,褴褛的,不适的
examples: Frank ran errands for a seedy local villain.
He found his brother in a seedy, roach-infested apartment.
Its many mortgage loans are looking increasingly seedy as the British economy turns down.
He went on from Vienna to study in Berlin, and was stimulated by its seedy social life.
